title: Panorra API Go New Project Report

issues.display_title: Issue #378: Refactor Likes Module to Use GORM Models
issues.main_description_html:
RESULT 30/06/26

✅ Implementation: Refactor Likes Module

This document details the refactoring process for the Likes module to adopt GORM models.

Phase 1: Models Preparation

  • Discovered that the like table did not have a corresponding GORM model.
  • Created src/Models/Like.go representing the like table.

Phase 2: Refactoring Endpoints

  • LikeComment.go:

    • Replaced Config.DB.Raw("SELECT id FROM like_comment...").Scan() with Config.DB.First(&Models.LikeComment{}).
    • Replaced Config.DB.Exec("INSERT INTO like_comment...") with Config.DB.Create(&Models.LikeComment{}).
    • Replaced Config.DB.Exec("DELETE FROM like_comment...") with Config.DB.Delete(&like).
    • Added UUID generation with uuid.New() and parsing with uuid.MustParse().

  • LikePost.go:

    • Replaced raw SQL queries for liking and unliking a post with Models.Like GORM operations (Config.DB.First, Config.DB.Create, Config.DB.Delete).
    • Replaced raw SQL query INSERT INTO notifications with Models.Notifications creation via Config.DB.Create.
    • Replaced raw SQL query INSERT INTO broadcast_containers and UPDATE broadcast_containers with Models.BroadcastContainers GORM operations.
    • Used json.Marshal to handle jsonb column serialization safely instead of relying on string queries.

Phase 3: Validation

  • Confirmed that go build ./src/Action/V1/Likes executes successfully without errors.

Background

The Likes module currently handles operations (liking a post, liking a comment) using manual SQL queries (tx.Raw, Config.DB.Exec) and maps for retrieving data. Transitioning to GORM models enforces type safety, avoids nil pointer panics on nullable database columns, and simplifies SQL injection prevention.

Objective

Refactor the API endpoints within src/Action/V1/Likes/ to utilize the existing GORM models (e.g., Models.Like, Models.LikeComment, Models.Post, Models.Comment, Models.Notification, Models.BroadcastContainer).

APIs to Refactor

  • LikePost: Refactor manual INSERT INTO "like", DELETE FROM "like", INSERT INTO notifications, and INSERT INTO broadcast_containers logic into standard GORM logic (e.g. Config.DB.Create(&Models.Like{})).
  • LikeComment: Refactor manual lookup (SELECT id FROM like_comment), insertion (INSERT INTO like_comment), and deletion (DELETE FROM like_comment) into standard GORM model methods (Config.DB.First, Config.DB.Create, Config.DB.Delete).

Acceptance Criteria

  • No raw SQL INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE queries remain in the refactored endpoints.
  • Explicitly generate and assign UUIDs using uuid.New() in Go before insertion to prevent zero-value constraint errors (if the models use UUID).
  • The code successfully compiles (go build) without any unused imports.

Source: docs/task/refactor_likes.md

issues.display_title: Issue #379: Refactor Auth Module to Use GORM Models
issues.main_description_html:
RESULT 30/06/26

✅ Implementation: Refactor Auth Module

This document outlines the refactoring process for the Auth module to replace raw SQL queries with GORM models.

Phase 1: Review Models

  • Verified Models.Otp, Models.User, Models.UsersRequestDelete, and Models.GothruAccount.
  • Found that Otp.Exp is a pointer to an int64 (*int64), meaning we could assign dynamic properties natively using GORM.

Phase 2: Refactoring Endpoints

  • Login.go:

    • Replaced manual OTP Config.DB.Table("otp") lookup, insert, and update logic with Config.DB.Select().Where().First(&otp), Config.DB.Create(&newOtp), and Config.DB.Model(&otp).Updates(...).
    • Replaced raw DELETE FROM users_request_delete with Config.DB.Where().Delete(&Models.UsersRequestDelete{}).
    • Replaced raw UPDATE users SET is_active = true, is_back = true with Config.DB.Model(&Models.User{}).Updates(...).
    • Replaced gothru_account lookups and raw inserts with Models.GothruAccount equivalents.
    • Eliminated the unused timeplus format string declaration to pass compilation successfully.

  • LoginApple.go:

    • Migrated the users_request_delete raw SQL DELETE and UPDATE users to standard GORM model manipulations for deactivated user recovery.

  • LoginGoogle.go:

    • Migrated the users_request_delete raw SQL DELETE and UPDATE users to standard GORM logic.
    • Migrated gothru_account insertion to use Config.DB.Create(&Models.GothruAccount{}).

Phase 3: Validation

  • Imported necessary packages (uuid, gorm, Models).
  • Ran go build ./src/Action/V1/Auth which finished with no errors.

Background

The Auth module (Login.go, LoginApple.go, LoginGoogle.go) currently manages authentication and OTP generation using raw SQL queries (tx.Raw, Config.DB.Exec) for checking and inserting OTPs, and gothru_account rows. Transitioning these to standard GORM models enforces type safety and maintains ORM convention consistency across the application.

Objective

Refactor the API endpoints within src/Action/V1/Auth/ to utilize standard GORM models (e.g., Models.Otp, Models.GothruAccount).

APIs to Refactor

  • Login.go: Replace INSERT INTO OTP, INSERT INTO GOTHRU ACCOUNT and any other raw SQL with GORM models.
  • LoginApple.go: Replace raw SQL OTP and user data manipulations with GORM.
  • LoginGoogle.go: Replace raw SQL OTP and gothru_account logic with GORM models.

Acceptance Criteria

  • No raw SQL INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE queries remain in the refactored endpoints.
  • Ensure UUIDs are safely created in Go with uuid.New() and parsed with uuid.MustParse() before insertion.
  • The code successfully compiles (go build) without any unused imports.

Source: docs/task/refactor_auth.md

issues.display_title: Issue #380: Refactor Report Module to Use GORM Models
issues.main_description_html:
RESULT 30/06/26

✅ Implementation: Refactor Report Module

This document outlines the refactoring process completed for the Report module to replace raw SQL queries with standard GORM models.

Phase 1: Models Preparation

  • Verified the existence and correctness of GORM models Models.ReportBug, Models.ReportIssueComment, and Models.ReportIssuePost.

Phase 2: Refactoring Endpoints

  • ReportBug.go:

    • Refactored Config.DB.Exec("INSERT INTO report_bug...") into Config.DB.Create(&Models.ReportBug{}).
    • Added UUID generation with uuid.New().

  • UpdateReportBug.go:

    • Refactored Config.DB.Exec("UPDATE report_bug SET...") into Config.DB.Model(&Models.ReportBug{}).Where(...).Updates(...).

  • ReportComment.go:

    • Changed raw SQL lookup Config.DB.Table("report_issue_comment").Select("id").Scan(...) to Config.DB.Select("id").First(&Models.ReportIssueComment{}).
    • Changed INSERT INTO report_issue_comment to Config.DB.Create(&Models.ReportIssueComment{}).
    • Changed UPDATE report_issue_comment to Config.DB.Model(&report).Updates(...).

  • ReportPost.go:

    • Changed raw SQL lookup Config.DB.Table("report_issue_post").Select("id").Scan(...) to Config.DB.Select("id").First(&Models.ReportIssuePost{}).
    • Changed INSERT INTO report_issue_post to Config.DB.Create(&Models.ReportIssuePost{}).
    • Changed UPDATE report_issue_post to Config.DB.Model(&report).Updates(...).

Phase 3: Cleanup and Validation

  • Removed unused imports (e.g. "time") from ReportComment.go and ReportPost.go.
  • Added missing imports for github.com/google/uuid, gorm.io/gorm, and panorra-api-new/src/Models.
  • Successfully validated that go build ./src/Action/V1/Report completes without any syntax or unused import errors.

Background

The Report module (ReportBug.go, UpdateReportBug.go, ReportComment.go, ReportPost.go) currently manages reporting bugs, updating bug statuses, and reporting comments/posts using raw SQL queries (tx.Raw, Config.DB.Exec) and maps. Transitioning these to standard GORM models enforces type safety, provides zero-value UUID checking, and maintains ORM convention consistency across the application.

Objective

Refactor the API endpoints within src/Action/V1/Report/ to utilize standard GORM models (e.g., Models.ReportBug, Models.ReportIssueComment, Models.ReportIssuePost, Models.Post, Models.Comment).

APIs to Refactor

  • ReportBug: Replace INSERT INTO report_bug with Config.DB.Create(&Models.ReportBug{}).
  • UpdateReportBug: Replace UPDATE report_bug with Config.DB.Model(&Models.ReportBug{}).Updates().
  • ReportComment: Replace manual lookups, INSERT INTO report_issue_comment, and UPDATE report_issue_comment with GORM equivalents (First, Create, Updates).
  • ReportPost: Replace manual lookups, INSERT INTO report_issue_post, and UPDATE report_issue_post with GORM equivalents (First, Create, Updates).

Acceptance Criteria

  • No raw SQL INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE queries remain in the refactored endpoints.
  • Ensure UUIDs are safely created in Go with uuid.New() and parsed with uuid.MustParse() before insertion.
  • The code successfully compiles (go build) without any unused imports.

Source: docs/task/refactor_report.md

issues.display_title: Issue #381: Refactor Register Module to Use GORM Models
issues.main_description_html:
RESULT 30/06/26

✅ Implementation: Refactor Register Module

This document details the transition from raw SQL insertions/updates to standard GORM model operations within the Register module.

Files Modified

  1. RegisterUser.go

    • Transformed raw SQL UPDATE users and UPDATE otp into Config.DB.Model(&Models.User{}).Updates(...) and Config.DB.Model(&Models.Otp{}).Updates(...) respectively.
    • Replaced raw SQL INSERT INTO users, user_detail, user_account, and otp with proper model initialization and Config.DB.Create(...).
    • Removed unused timestamp variables (timeplus).

  2. RegisterApple.go

    • Refactored raw SQL insertions for users, user_detail, and user_account by utilizing the respective Models.User, Models.UserDetail, and Models.UserAccount structures.

  3. RegisterGoogle.go

    • Refactored raw SQL insertions for users, user_detail, and user_account similar to the Apple registration flow.

  4. ValidateRegister.go

    • Verified that no raw SQL insertions or updates are present in this file (only query selects which are fine to stay or already GORM).

  5. RegistrationFunctions.go (Utils)

    • Created GenerateDefaultUserDetail(userId uuid.UUID) Models.UserDetail to centralize the creation of default UserDetail models. This removed code duplication across all registration endpoints.

Highlights

  • Safely generated UUIDs in Go side for new users via uuid.New().
  • Extracted UserDetail defaults into a centralized function GenerateDefaultUserDetail, cleaning up repetitive code in the handlers.
  • Successfully eliminated unneeded package imports and verified 100% compilation success for go build ./src/Action/V1/Register.

Background

The Register module (RegisterUser.go, RegisterApple.go, RegisterGoogle.go, ValidateRegister.go) performs insertions into users, user_detail, user_account, and otp tables using raw SQL queries (tx.Raw, Config.DB.Exec). These raw queries need to transition into standard GORM model operations.

Objective

Refactor the API endpoints within src/Action/V1/Register/ to utilize standard GORM models (e.g., Models.User, Models.UserDetail, Models.UserAccount, Models.Otp).

APIs to Refactor

  • RegisterUser.go: Replace INSERT INTO users, INSERT INTO user_detail, INSERT INTO user_account, and INSERT INTO otp logic with Config.DB.Create(...).
  • RegisterApple.go: Replace raw SQL insertions with GORM models.
  • RegisterGoogle.go: Replace raw SQL insertions with GORM models.
  • ValidateRegister.go: Check if any raw SQL insertions or updates exist, and if so, refactor them.
  • Refactor UserDetail: Extract the logic for generating default UserDetail into a shared function GenerateDefaultUserDetail in RegistrationFunctions.go.

Acceptance Criteria

  • No raw SQL INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE queries remain in the refactored endpoints.
  • Ensure UUIDs are safely created in Go with uuid.New() and parsed with uuid.MustParse() before insertion.
  • The code successfully compiles (go build ./src/Action/V1/Register) without any unused imports.

Source: docs/task/refactor_register.md
